title: Walvis Bay Boat Rental Guide: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Nestled on the Atlantic coast, Walvis Bay offers sheltered waters perfect for a self-drive motorboat or a crewed yacht. Renting a boat here typically costs from €100 for a shared dolphin tour to €1,200 for a full-day luxury catamaran, with most trips leaving from the Walvis Bay Yacht Club pontoon. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Walvis Bay or list your own.

Where to set off in Walvis Bay

The primary departure point is the Walvis Bay Waterfront, adjacent to the lagoon. Nearly all charters begin from the public jetty near the Raft Restaurant. For private yachts, the Walvis Bay Yacht Club provides visitor berths, while larger vessels use the Walvis Bay Port Marina, which offers full services. The Pelican Point sand spit, reached via a 30-minute boat ride, is a favoured anchorage for seal colonies. Nearby Swakopmund, 30 km north, also has a small jetty, but the harbour there is shallow and less reliable. You can yacht charter Walvis Bay for your chosen dates.

Guided trips often include the bird-rich lagoon, a Ramsar site with thousands of flamingos. For a longer voyage, heading south to Sandwich Harbour (about 15 nautical miles) reveals vast dunes meeting the Atlantic. When you book a boat, check whether the itinerary includes these natural landmarks.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size and whether you want to drive yourself or hire a skipper. For a first-time visitor, a half-day catamaran cruise is the safest option.

  1. Dual-consul kayak or small motorboat best for solo explorers or couples, maximum 2 guests, from €80 for a shared tour.
  2. 4.5-metre centre-console ideal for a pair wanting to fish the lagoon, capacity 3 people, from €150 for a self-drive half-day.
  3. 8-metre cabin cruiser suited for families of up to 6, includes a basic galley, from €350 for a full-day charter with a skipper.
  4. 12-metre catamaran (Lagoon 40 or similar) perfect for a group of 8 to 10, offering deck lounges and a shaded cockpit, from €900 for a full-day trip including lunch.
  5. 14-metre motor yacht (Fairline Targa 43) the premium pick for up to 10 guests on multi-day voyages, with overnight cabins, from €1,200 per day. See the Fairline listing for a complete specification.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Walvis Bay?

Prices range from €80 for a shared tour to €1,200 for a crewed luxury yacht. The exact amount depends on boat size, duration, and season. Below is a typical 2026 price breakdown. One option worth a look is the Fairline listing.

  • Shared dolphin and seal tour (2 hours): €80 per person, departing twice daily from the Waterfront.
  • Small motorboat self-drive (half-day, 4 hours): €150 to €250 depending on engine size.
  • Catamaran full-day private charter (6-8 hours): €600 to €900 for a boat like a Lagoon 380.
  • Crewed motor yacht with skipper (full day, up to 10 guests): €1,000 to €1,200, often including fuel and lunch.

For the best selection of boats, browse the yacht charter Walvis Bay options on BoatHire24, where all listings include transparent pricing and verified owner profiles.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Walvis Bay?

You do not need a licence for a crewed charter, but a local permit is required to drive a self-drive boat. In Namibia, inland waters like the lagoon are not covered by international licence endorsements; the rental operator confirms exact requirements at booking. A short on-water briefing and a basic competency check are standard.

"For self-drive boats, always ask the owner about wind patterns. The south-westerly 'Berg Wind' can pick up abruptly after 14:00 in summer, so plan to return by early afternoon."

The best season for reliable weather is from November to April (the Namibian summer), when the coastal fog is least frequent and daytime temperatures hover around 25°C. The 'Berg Wind' is a hot, dry offshore wind that can blow during July and August, reducing visibility but keeping seas calm.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 to €1,200 per day
  • Peak season: November to April
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day (8 hours)
  • Maximum guests: up to 10 on a crewed yacht
  • Currency: EUR (prices approximate; owners may use NAD or ZAR)

Questions about boat rental in Walvis Bay

Can I rent a boat for a day trip to Sandwich Harbour?

Yes, several operators offer day trips from Walvis Bay to Sandwich Harbour, a 15-nautical-mile journey south, often combined with dune driving on the return.

What is the cancellation policy for boat rentals in Walvis Bay?

Most owners on BoatHire24 offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure, but always check the specific listing terms, especially for crewed charters.

Are there any age restrictions for passengers?

Children are welcome on most boats, but life jackets must be worn at all times; infants under 12 months are not recommended on open skiffs due to safety regulations.

Is it possible to book a boat with a skipper for just a few hours?

A minimum booking of four hours is typical for crewed charters, though some skippers accept shorter trips for a fixed rate of €250 for two hours.

Whether you want a quick lagoon excursion or a multi-day voyage along the Skeleton Coast, Walvis Bay offers some of the most scenic boating in southern Africa. Boat owners can easily list their vessel on BoatHire24, reaching thousands of travellers planning their 2026 trips. Visit the platform to compare verified boats and join the community.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Walvis Bay and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
